segments.s182-g17: چو is a subordinate conjunction meaning “when/if.” این داستان سربسر is the object phrase: این = “this,” داستان = “story/tale,” and سربسر is an adverbial compound meaning “throughout/from head to tail” that qualifies how the tale is heard. بشنوی is the 2nd-person singular present-subjunctive (poetic/conditional use) of شنیدن, here used with چو to mean “you hear / if you hear.”
segments.s182-g18: ببینی is the 2nd-person singular present-subjunctive of دیدن used as the result clause after چو, so “you will see / you will find.” The verb appears before its complement in this poetic line. سر مایه is a noun phrase meaning “chief/source” (سر as “chief/top” modifying مایه “cause/resource”), and بدخوی is an adjective after the noun meaning “ill-tempered,” describing that source.
segments.s182-g9: سپهبد is the subject 'the spahbed'. چو is the temporal/conditional particle 'when'/'if' introducing the clause. خواند is the past tense verb 'called/summoned' (here 'call' as in summon). ورا is the object pronoun 'him' (classical word order places object after verb). دوستدار is a predicate nominal 'a lover/one who loves' that will be negated in the next segment (the copula/negation follows in the next clause).
segments.s182-g10: نباشد is the negative present/subjunctive form 'is not / would not be' (classical Persian uses this after conditional clauses); خرد is the noun 'wisdom' and functions as the thing that 'is not'. با دلش سازگار is a prepositional phrase 'in harmony with his heart' (با = 'with', دلش = 'his heart', سازگار = 'harmonious/agreeing'), completing the predication 'wisdom is not in harmony with his heart'.
segments.s182-g11: گرش here is a contracted/poetic form (گر + ش) and functions as the conditional 'if (him/he)' — none of the listed dictionary senses match that grammatical use exactly. زآرزو = 'from/out of desire/longing' marks what he is kept from. بازدارد is a present/subjunctive or habitual 'restrains/keeps back'. سپهر (here placed after the verb) is 'the sky/heavens' and is the agent doing the restraining.
segments.s182-g12: همان is a demonstrative 'that/the same' pointing back to the antecedent; آفرینش here means 'praise/that which is praised' or 'the sung praise' rather than 'creation' (context and the following verb favor 'praise'/'song'). نخواند is the negative present/future-like form of 'خواندن' meaning 'will not sing/perform' (here 'will not be sung'). بمهر is an adverbial phrase 'with affection/with love' (بـ + مهر).
segments.s182-g13: ورا = 'to him'. هیچ خوبی = 'no good/none of goodness'. نخواهد here functions as a negative future/modal auxiliary 'will not (be/come/occur)'; Persian often uses خواستن/نخاستن idiomatically this way. به دل is the locative/experiential phrase 'to the heart' (i.e. 'pleasing to his heart' or 'enter his heart'), so the clause means 'no good will (be) to his heart' — i.e. nothing good will please or befall him.
segments.s182-g14: شود is the present/future auxiliary 'becomes / will be'. آرزوهای او is the subject 'his desires' (آرزوهای = plural 'desires/wishes', او = 'his'). دلگسل is a predicative adjective 'heart‑rending / heart‑breaking' describing the effect those desires have on him: 'his desires will rend his heart.'
segments.s182-g15: و 'and' connects to the previous thought. دیگر کش is a poetic/orthographic variant for 'دیگر کس' = 'no other person/another'; the provided lemma 'کش' does not list the ordinary sense 'person', so it is treated as an irregular/archaic spelling here. از بن = 'from the root' (i.e. innate/in the depths). نباشد خرد = 'there is no wisdom' or 'wisdom will not be' — the negative copula applies to خرد (wisdom). Overall: 'and no one else will have wisdom from the root.'
segments.s182-g16: خردمندش is 'his wise one' / 'his wise person' (خردمند + -ش 'his') and here functions as the agent; از مردمان introduces the group 'of/among men' and نشمرد is the negative present of 'شماریدن' meaning 'will not count/consider'. Put together: 'his (own) wisest will not count him among (true) people' — i.e. the wise will not reckon him as one of the people (because of lack of wisdom).
segments.s182-g1: جهانجوی (subject) + چون شد (temporal clause) + سرافراز (predicate adjective) و گرد. چـون here functions as a temporal 'when' introducing the clause; شد is the past/become sense of شدن. سرافراز is an adjectival predicate describing the subject. و links to گرد, which completes the predicate (گرد here participates in the following compound with سپه). Word order follows the poetic sequence Subject — temporal verb phrase — predicate.
segments.s182-g2: This is the main clause that completes the previous temporal clause: سپه را is the definite object marked by را; بدشمن is the recipient-like noun phrase describing the person to whom the army would be given; نشاید is an impersonal/archaic modal meaning 'it is not fitting' or 'ought not'; سپرد is the past infinitive/past form of سپردن 'to entrust/hand over'. Together: 'it is not fitting to entrust the army to an evil enemy.'
segments.s182-g3: سرشک is the noun 'teardrop' and اندر آید is a poetic phrase 'comes into/appears'; بمژگان ('by/on the eyelashes') specifies the location; ز + رشک gives the source 'from jealousy'. Note the Verb آید follows the subject (poetic word order) and اندر is an archaic preposition meaning 'in/into'.
segments.s182-g4: This is a relative clause modifying the tear: سرشکی 'a teardrop' + که 'which/that' introduces the clause. درمان is the noun 'remedy/cure'; نداند is the negative present/archaic third-person form 'does not know'; پزشک 'physician' is the subject placed at the end (verb before subject is common in poetry). The clause means 'a tear for which no physician knows a cure.'
segments.s182-g5: کسی 'someone' + کز (archaic contraction of که از) marks the relative 'who/that from' and introduces the origin: نژاد بزرگان 'the race/lineage of the great ones'. بود is the past/copular 'was'. The whole is a relative clause: 'someone who was from the lineage of the great ones.'
segments.s182-g6: بماند is the verb 'remained/continued' (here used in narrative sense). به بیشی literally 'with excess/more' indicates superiority or being at the top; سترگ in this context is used adjectivally ('lofty' / 'exalted sight' in poetic usage) and آن بود closes with the copula 'that was'. The clause states the resulting status: 'he remained loftier/more exalted.'
segments.s182-g7: چو 'when' introduces a conditional/temporal clause. بی‌کام دل بنده is a noun phrase 'the servant's/lover's heart being unfulfilled' (بی‌کام modifies دل). باید is the modal auxiliary 'must/it is necessary' and بدن ('the body') is the noun that will be affected by the required action; the full verb phrase completes in the next segment. Note the poetic compactness where the modal precedes the complement.
segments.s182-g8: بکام کسی 'to someone's desire/for someone's pleasure' modifies the infinitival action that follows; داستانها is the object 'stories/tales'; زدن here is an idiomatic verbal use meaning 'to tell/narrate' (زدن often functions with abstract objects to mean 'to perform or produce' that action). Together: 'one must tell stories to please someone' or literally 'to strike/perform stories to someone's desire.'

sentences.s182-s9: When you hear this whole tale through, you will see that its chief source is ill-temper.
sentences.s182-s5: When the spahbed called him, if he was not a lover, wisdom would not be in harmony with his heart.
sentences.s182-s6: If the heavens keep him back from longing, that very creation will not sing with love.
sentences.s182-s7: No good will come to him; his desires will rend his heart.
sentences.s182-s8: And no other person will have wisdom at the root; even his wisest will not reckon him among men.
sentences.s182-s1: When the ambitious one became triumphant and chief, the army should not be entrusted to an evil enemy.
sentences.s182-s2: A teardrop wells into the eyelashes from jealousy; a tear for which no physician knows a cure.
sentences.s182-s3: Someone who was of the lineage of the great ones remained loftier than others.
sentences.s182-s4: When the servant's heart is unfulfilled, the body must be given to someone's desire; one must tell stories to please someone.
